combined_cases.description:
As- Salam - Alikum,

I lost job in Feb 2026 and I am already having lendings as well. I trying to fix Job issue but I cant find one after that. Is It possible can you please hep me in this?

Thanks
Regards
Sehrish
combined_cases.ai_summary: Individual needs financial assistance after job loss with existing debts, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description:
Dear Zakat Foundation,

I hope this message finds you well.

My name is Jamila, and I am currently in my fourth year in Australia. I arrived in 2023 and got married shortly after. I applied for a spouse visa, and during this time I was blessed with two young children—one is now 2 years old and the other is 9 months old.

About a year into the marriage, my husband and I separated. Alhamdulillah, I have remained patient through this situation. Since our separation, he had been supporting me by helping with rent and some basic living expenses while I awaited the outcome of my spouse visa, which would allow me to apply for Centrelink assistance.

Unfortunately, he has recently informed me that he is no longer able to continue supporting me financially due to his own financial difficulties and he can’t pay rent anymore. At the moment, I am not working, I do not receive Centrelink support, and I do not have any family here I can stay with.

I am therefore in a very difficult situation and urgently need assistance with rent and basic necessities for myself and my children until my visa is processed and I become eligible for Centrelink support. I am doing my best to remain strong, but I am deeply concerned about the possibility of becoming homeless with my children.

I would be sincerely grateful for any support or guidance you may be able to offer during this challenging time.

Thank you very much for your time and understanding.

Jizakaallah ,Jamila
combined_cases.ai_summary: Mother with two young children requires support during visa application process, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description:
I'm Yusniza Yusof, I hope this message finds you well.
I am writing to humbly seek assistance from your zakat organization in Australia to help settle the outstanding debt of my late husband, Akram bin Hamdi.

The debt, amounting to approximately AUD 4,000+, is owed to ZipPay and ZipMoney. This financial burden began in 2023 when my husband was diagnosed with cancer. Following medical advice from his oncologist, we made the difficult decision to return to Malaysia for his treatment and care. Due to these circumstances, the payments for the Zip accounts were left unsettled.

Sadly, my husband passed away on 6 March 2026. As his surviving spouse, I am now facing challenges in managing this debt.

I sincerely hope for your consideration and support in assisting with this matter through zakat funds. Your help would greatly ease this burden during this difficult time.
Thank you for your time, understanding, and compassion. I am willing to provide any supporting documents if required.
combined_cases.ai_summary: Widow seeks assistance settling deceased husband's outstanding debt of over $4000, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description: Asalamualiykum, my name is Abdullah and I am in urgent need of support due to a series of overwhelming hardships. My parents are divorced, and my father has left the country, refusing to pay any child support, leaving my family without any income. I am just 17 years old, in the middle of my HSC studies, and unable to work. My mother also cannot work, as she is the sole caregiver for my autistic brother. Adding to this, my father was physically abusive to my mother, causing her serious injuries that have left her unable to work. Now, we are on the brink of eviction, I am already three weeks behind on rent, and if I don’t pay soon, I will lose our home, and honestlyits really hard to soend money towards groceries, food, tuitions and daily essentials as rent is always on my mind and that i have to keeo saving money for to oay it off over week as most if the time i have ni choice but to sleep and wake uo hungry. I am desperate for help, as I have no income source and no way to avoid this imminent crisis. I need financial assistance immediately, by today or tomorrow, to keep a roof over our heads and continue my education.
combined_cases.ai_summary: 17-year-old student needs urgent support due to absent father and family income loss, high urgency.

combined_cases.description: Abdurazzaq struggles with daily living specialists, medication, transportation, food. Abdulrazzaq has heart problem, strong discs in his spinal and neck not allowing him to work in addition of his age (66 years old). Abdulrazzaq receives Centrelink payment.
combined_cases.ai_summary: Elderly man with health conditions needs daily living support despite receiving Centrelink, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description: Married couple on a student VISA, recently gave birth to baby Jannatul (unplanned) who is 6 months old now. Triple X syndrome detected in early pregnancy. Financial hardship following birth of baby, needing to purchase day to day goods
combined_cases.ai_summary: Student visa couple with special needs baby facing financial hardship, needs daily essentials, high urgency.

combined_cases.description:
I am currently based in Kuala Lumpur to stay with my wife who has had to return to Malaysia. In the last year I have incurred a large medical bill and other debts and an ongoing effort to pay for it. However, I have not been able to keep up with my promises to pay for the debt in the instalments. The matter has been with the bailiff’s office and over the past 4 months they have been kindly giving me extension to pay but I have not been able to.
This has led to now being given time til today 22 April to pay minimum RM4500 (AU1600 approx) as an instalment towards the full debt of RM10,250 (AU3620) otherwise the bailiff’s office will proceed to confine me starting with weekend detention until full payment is made. This may also lead to being filed as bankrupt and this might also lead to cancellation of my visa here to stay with my wife. I also have two debts for personal loan I took from two individuals total RM8000 (AU2800 approx) for over nearly 9 months and have to pay this too because they are asking me to settle this immediately.
I went to the bailiff’s office Thursday 16 April last week, advising them that the Zakat Foundation would be able to assist me, and so they have now given me til today Wednesday 22nd next week, otherwise they will confine me from Friday. i had provided all this information last week immediately after Thursday 16th to Zakat Foundation. I have explained the complete situation to brother Munir in detail who has been very understanding, Barak Allahu fik.
combined_cases.ai_summary: Individual overseas with wife needs help managing accumulated medical bills and debts, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description: I need help with paying bond for a rental property of amount $2360
combined_cases.ai_summary: Person requires assistance with rental bond payment of $2360, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description: انا كنت اعمل الفترة الي فاتت بمستودع ولاكن فصلت بدوامي و لدي عائلتي هنا و انا على طلب فيزا الحماية 866 ، السنترالينك قالو لن يقومو بالدفع لي و ايضا سوف ابداء عملي الجديد الاسبوع القادم ولي لدي صعوبة بالايجار المتاخر
combined_cases.ai_summary: Protection visa applicant with family needs help with overdue rent, starting new job, high urgency.

combined_cases.description:
Assalymu Alaykum, due to my son’s ongoing health conditions I had to leave my job; two months ago. During this time my son has been frequently hospitalized. As a result of not being able to work and not being able to have an income my 3 children and I are now facing eviction. 

I would be immensely grateful for any assistance available. JazakAllah Khira.
combined_cases.ai_summary: Parent who left job for child's health needs facing eviction with three children, high urgency.

combined_cases.description:
As salamu alaikum :)
I am in need of 2 weeks advance rent if possible. 
I also am in need of a washing machine & dryer please.

I sent an application when I was still homeless with my son, but we have signed a lease and moved in now. 
We are just missing a few basic/ essentials needed for our new home.
I can send further documents such lease, bank statements etc
Thank you.
combined_cases.ai_summary: Previously homeless parent needs advance rent and essential household appliances for new home, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description:
Dear brothers and sisters,
Alsalamu allikum,

I hope this message finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request financial assistance through your support programs, as I am currently experiencing significant and ongoing financial hardship.

For the past two years, I have been struggling financially and have accumulated debts through loans from friends as well as services such as ZipPay and Afterpay. My financial situation worsened after I lost my job approximately one year ago, and I remained unemployed for about five months. Despite my efforts to recover and manage my obligations, I am still finding it very difficult to meet essential expenses.

I am currently preparing for my Pharmacy Board Intern Exam in Australia, which is a crucial step for me to become a registered pharmacist and secure stable employment. As part of this process, I am required to pay AUD 700 for the PSA exam. Unfortunately, I am unable to afford this fee due to my current financial situation.

This exam represents a very important turning point in my life. Successfully completing it will allow me to finally stand on solid ground, gain stable employment, and become financially independent.

I am a single woman living in Australia without family or support, and I am doing my best to improve my situation and build a secure future. I would be truly grateful if you could consider supporting me at this critical stage.

I am more than willing to provide any required evidence of exam registration and fees with Pharmaceutical Society of Australia.

Thank you very much for your time, understanding, and the meaningful support you provide to those in need.

Thank you very much,
Fatma Sharaf
combined_cases.ai_summary: Individual experiencing ongoing two-year financial hardship seeks general assistance, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description:
I am writing to support an application for emergency relief on behalf of my client, Taybeh, a recently arrived refugee and single mother  of 4 children currently adjusting to life in Australia with her children.
Taybeh became the sole carer for her children following the passing of her husband in 2024. Since arriving in Australia, she has faced significant challenges settling into a new environment, including navigating unfamiliar systems, limited social supports, and ongoing financial hardship.
Taybeh is currently experiencing acute financial stress, with little to no income and difficulty meeting basic living expenses such as food, household needs, and essential bills. In an effort to improve her situation and become financially independent, she purchased a sewing machine with borrowed funds, hoping to generate income through sewing work. However, she has not yet been able to establish this income stream and is now in debt, which she is struggling to repay.
In addition to financial pressures, Taybeh is supporting her children through their own adjustment challenges, including health and wellbeing concerns that are currently being addressed with appropriate services.
Emergency relief is critical at this time to assist Taybeh in meeting her family’s immediate needs while she continues to settle and work towards stability. Without this support, the family remains at risk of ongoing financial hardship and difficulty maintaining basic living standards.

I strongly recommend that this application be considered favourably.
combined_cases.ai_summary: Recently arrived refugee single mother of four needs emergency relief support, high urgency.

combined_cases.description:
Assalamualaikum,
I am requesting Zakat assistance for my family. We are currently facing serious financial hardship. I have six children, and only my husband is working. Our expenses are very high, and we are struggling to meet basic needs.
 We urgently need support with:
- Food assistance
- Emergency financial relief
- Car repairs and registration renewal
- Replacement or repair of essential household items (washing machine, television)

Support with housing, as the owner has asked us to leave our current home

Our situation is very difficult, and we are doing our best to manage, but we cannot cope without help. Any Zakat or financial assistance you can provide would mean a lot to us.

Please let me know what documents you need from me.

JazakAllahu khairan,
Nurul
combined_cases.ai_summary: Family with six children needs urgent food and emergency assistance due to financial hardship, high urgency.

combined_cases.description: Food voucher and fuel voucher please

combined_cases.ai_summary: Post-surgery diabetic person needs food and fuel vouchers for medical dietary requirements, moderate urgency.

combined_cases.description:
I'm assisting Saleheh in completing this application as she requested support(I'm her case manager from SSI), Saleheh is a single mother with three children (25-21-16)and looking after her elderly mother(77), the family arrived in Australia on 7/11/2026, and the English language is limitted and they are all except the elderly lady learning English now and they rely on Centrelink income and they are facing financial hardship due to multiple health appointment for Saleheg and his elderly mother, Hopefuly they can get some support from your sid,
Please let me know if you require further information
Munira Mitho
mmitho@ssi.org.au
0422337984
combined_cases.ai_summary: Recently arrived single mother with three children and elderly mother needs comprehensive support, high urgency.

combined_cases.description:
I formally authorise Ms Rasa Aziz to act on my behalf and represent me in all matters relating to this application, as I am unable to communicate effectively in English and require assistance


I am an Afghan mother of four children currently living in a rental property. I have been residing in Australia for approximately two years. One of my children, aged 7, has a disability and requires additional care and support.

I am currently experiencing significant financial hardship across multiple areas of my life. I have limited English proficiency, as my primary languages are Dari and Pashto, which has made it very difficult for me to access services, secure stable support, and communicate effectively. I do not drive and do not have access to a vehicle, which further limits my ability to manage daily responsibilities and attend appointments.

At present, I am facing several urgent issues. My refrigerator has broken down, and I am unable to afford a replacement, which is impacting my ability to safely store food for my children. One of my daughters is unwell, and I have multiple upcoming medical expenses in early May. While some costs are partially covered by Medicare, I am still required to pay a significant amount out of pocket, which I cannot afford.

In addition, I am struggling to pay my electricity bills and urgently require pest control assistance, as my home is currently affected by a severe cockroach infestation. I am also unable to afford basic necessities such as groceries, clothing, and shoes for my children.

My rent is extremely high at $1,270 per fortnight, which places a significant financial burden on me. I have been actively trying to secure more suitable and affordable two-bedroom accommodation; however, my applications have been repeatedly declined due to having four children.

Overall, I am in a very difficult financial situation and am struggling to meet even the most basic needs for my family. I respectfully request urgent financial assistance and support services to help stabilise our living conditions and ensure the health, safety, and wellbeing of my children.
combined_cases.ai_summary: Afghan mother of four children in rental property requires assistance with communication barriers, moderate urgency.

ai_analysis.quality_summary: Nearly all 60 cases are assigned 'No Priority' despite many describing urgent situations requiring immediate P1/P2 intervention. Multiple cases show homelessness, domestic violence, eviction notices, and families with no food or income - all requiring same-day crisis response.
